hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W, F, Y) will enhance complementarity with SSTR2-specific ECL2/ECL3/TM5/TM6 regions, while mutating position 11 to hydrophobic residues (L, I, V) will reduce off-target (SSTR3/SSTR5) binding by avoiding unfavorable electrostatic interactions, thereby increasing selectivity margin.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current hypothesis remains valid: SSTR2 selectivity is achievable with strong pharmacophore preservation (FWKT7-10 and Cys3/Cys14) and disruption of conserved off-target contacts. However, structural stability must be improved to ensure viable candidates.

proposed_changes.rationale: Current clash threshold is too lenient, allowing unstable conformations. Tightening the clash filter will improve structural quality and reduce false positives.
A larger candidate pool increases the chances of generating structurally viable and selective designs, especially when structural filters are tightened.
